title: E2E UI + visual parity (Playwright e2e-browser)
against: ORIGINAL (pristine TS/Electron freshell); the frontend is retained UNCHANGED in the Rust port, so these specs + visual baselines are the T3 contract

how_produced.isolation: Each Playwright worker booted its OWN isolated server on an ephemeral loopback port via TestServer (isolated $HOME). The user live server on :3001 was never touched.
how_produced.flake_classification: Every failure was re-run in isolation at --workers=1 --retries=1 to separate flaky-under-contention from hard-against-original.

visual_baselines.note: The 6 screenshot-baselines assertions use maxDiffPixelRatio:0.05 and all MATCH on this host. editor-pane-loaded uses NO tolerance and mismatches by 118px (ratio 0.0100) — a baseline-strictness finding, not a functional regression.

baseline_semantics.reference: This exact 122-pass / 16-fail set is the T3 equivalence reference.
baseline_semantics.port_must: Keep all 122 currently-GREEN tests green (a newly-RED test is a PORT_DEFECT) and keep the 6 matching visual baselines matching.
baseline_semantics.port_need_not: Pass the 16 tests already RED against the original on this host (red==red is EQUIVALENT). Making any of them green is a candidate DELIBERATE_FIX (antagonist-adjudicated, ledgered).
baseline_semantics.why_red: All 16 reproduce against the PRISTINE original — pre-existing red, consistent with DESIGN.md: CI runs NONE of these suites, so they have rotted unnoticed. They are FINDINGS, not port failures.

core_in_baseline.description: Fully-green spec files against the original (the stable T3 CORE the port must reproduce).

covers_required_core.restart_recovery_note: server-restart-recovery + the *-restart-recovery provider specs are RED against the original — quarantined below (not a stable-CORE gate).

quarantined.description: Tests that FAIL against the pristine ORIGINAL on this host (findings, not port gates). Not force-greened; baselines were not retaken.

items.title: loads the editor lazily and requests a new JS asset after the click
items.evidence: toHaveScreenshot(editor-pane-loaded.png): 118px differ (ratio 0.0100). This assertion has NO maxDiffPixelRatio tolerance, unlike the 6 screenshot-baselines (0.05) which all MATCH. Reproduced isolated@1w + retry.

items.title: normalizes remote legacy layout sync before exposing server pane snapshots
items.evidence: normalizes remote legacy layout sync before exposing server pane snapshots; failed in 4-worker full run.

items.title: keeps fresh-agent settings and routes while legacy settings and routes are removed
items.evidence: keeps fresh-agent settings/routes while legacy settings/routes are removed; failed in 4-worker full run (19s).

items.title: restores Freshopencode turns from DB history when export is truncated
items.evidence: restores Freshopencode turns from truncated DB export; own TestServer + opencode DB fixtures.

items.title: does not materialize Freshopencode from DB rows without top-level run sessionID
items.evidence: does not materialize Freshopencode from DB rows without top-level run sessionID; own TestServer + opencode DB fixtures.

items.title: shows MRU tiles, sorted modal sources, and client-side filtering
items.evidence: MRU tiles / sorted modal sources / filtering; needs the opencode model catalog; failed in full run.

items.title: permission banner buttons are visible and functional on mobile
items.evidence: toContainText on the permission-banner card failed; hard-fail isolated@1w both attempts (17-18s each).

items.title: reconnecting second viewer keeps page 1 PTY size stable and both pages keep shared output
items.evidence: reconnecting 2nd viewer keeps page-1 PTY size stable + shared output; uses serverInfo.homeDir (co-located). Hard-fail isolated@1w both attempts (~25s).

items.title: enables multi-row tabs via settings toggle
items.evidence: multi-row-tabs switch toBeVisible failed after opening Settings; hard-fail isolated@1w both attempts. Sibling tests :19/:33 (harness-dispatch) PASS.

items.title: reattaches a UI-created OpenCode pane across browser refresh
items.evidence: reattaches a UI-created OpenCode pane across browser refresh; real opencode + own server.

items.title: recovers a hidden OpenCode sessionRef when association lands while the browser is closed
items.evidence: recovers a hidden OpenCode sessionRef when association lands while browser closed.

items.title: preserves an associated OpenCode pane across browser refresh
items.evidence: preserves an associated OpenCode pane across browser refresh (53s).

items.title: restores surviving OpenCode panes after graceful server restart and leaves a closed pane closed
items.evidence: restores surviving OpenCode panes after graceful server restart and leaves a closed pane closed.

items.title: restores multiple OpenCode panes after hard server kill
items.evidence: restores multiple OpenCode panes after hard server kill (32s).

items.title: freshclaude panes transition from waiting to blue running and back to idle
items.evidence: freshclaude tab icon toHaveClass(/text-blue-500/) failed; hard-fail isolated@1w both attempts. Siblings :33 (browser) and :185 (claude terminals) PASS.

items.title: all panes recover after server restart without rate limit errors
items.evidence: spawns then restarts its OWN TestServer on the same port; multi-pane recovery toPass timed out (30s). Hard-fail isolated@1w both attempts (~1.7m each).

flaky_under_contention.title: editor pane has path input and open button
flaky_under_contention.evidence: PASSED isolated@1w and in the 4-worker full run, but FAILED once at 6-worker contention (CORE run). Contention-sensitive, not a hard failure. In-baseline.

targetable_seam.purpose: Point the identical e2e specs at an arbitrary running server (the Rust port) instead of booting a local TestServer, so the port is graded by the same suite + visual goldens.

env_vars.FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_URL: http(s) base URL of the running server to grade (REQUIRED to enable external mode)
env_vars.FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_TOKEN: auth token the specs navigate with (?token=...)
env_vars.FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_HOME: optional: the target HOME dir if co-located, so serverInfo.homeDir specs work
env_vars.FRESHELL_E2E_SKIP_BUILD: optional: reuse existing dist for a local run (oracle config only)

targetable_seam.default_unchanged: When FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_URL is unset, the fixture spawns a normal TestServer — behavior identical to before the seam.
